title: Bridge Essay: Negative impact of daily screen use on inhibitory control network in preadolescence: A two-year follow-up study.
concept: Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.

**Negative impact of daily screen use on inhibitory control network in preadolescence: A two-year follow-up study.** - Dev Cogn Neurosci (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.dcn.2023.101218. PMID: 36821878. The COVID-19 pandemic has made an unprecedented shift in children's daily lives. Children are increasingly spending time with screens to learn and connect with others. As the online environment rapidly substitutes in-person experience, understanding children's neuropsychological trajectories associated with screen experiences is important.
